messages.content: example of three or four word pattern subject + verb + object with the word contrast as the verb

1. Apples contrast with oranges.
2. Black contrasts with white.
3. Daytime contrasts with nighttime.
4. Warm contrasts with cold.
grammatical structure for: apples contrast with oranges
Subject (apples) + verb (contrast) + object (oranges)

is contrast used as a verb in the following:If you show the contrast between two things …
1) Are you pointing out their differences?(yes)
2) Are you show how they are the same?(no)
3)Are you comparing their differences?(yes)

1) Are you pointing out their differences? (yes)
2) Are you showing how they are the same? (no)
3) Are you comparing their differences? (yes)

Yes, "contrast" is used as a verb in the sentence "If you show the contrast between two things…". The sentence implies that the two things being compared have differences that are being highlighted or pointed out, which means that option 1 and 3 are correct. Option 2 is not correct as the word "contrast" means to compare the differences between two things, not to show how they are the same.
grammatical structure of: the facts will emerge
Subject (the facts) + modal verb (will) + verb (emerge)
grammatical structure of: it has emerged that
Subject (it) + verb (has emerged) + conjunction (that)
grammatical structure: emerge from the dark
Verb (emerge) + preposition (from) + article + adjective (dark)
three or four word example of subject, verb, object pattern with emerge as the verb

1. Sun has emerged.
2. Truth will emerge.
3. Butterfly emerges beautifully. 
4. Ideas emerge naturally.
grammatical structure of: ideas emerge naturally
Subject (ideas) + verb (emerge) + adverb (naturally)
grammatical structure: facts will emerge in time
Subject (facts) + modal verb (will) + verb (emerge) + preposition (in) + noun (time)
four word example of emerge as a verb followed by an object

1. Sun emerging slowly.
2. Truth emerged suddenly.
3. Light emerged gradually.
4. Opportunities emerging daily.
grammatical structure of: opportunities emerge daily
Subject (opportunities) + verb (emerge) + adverb (daily)
